To find the value of \( A_{25} \), we first need to find a pattern in the product \( A_{n} \).

We are given that
$$
A_{n}=\prod_{k=1}^{n} \frac{(4 k-3) f(4 k-3)}{(4 k-1) f(4 k-1)}
$$

We can start by evaluating the function \( f(x) \) at \( x = 4k-3 \) and \( x = 4k-1 \) to see if there is a pattern.

$$
f(4k-3) = (4k-3)^5 - 3(4k-3)^3 + 2(4k-3)^2 + 3(4k-3) + 6
$$

$$
f(4k-1) = (4k-1)^5 - 3(4k-1)^3 + 2(4k-1)^2 + 3(4k-1) + 6
$$

We can simplify the expression for \( A_{n} \) by canceling out common factors in the numerator and denominator.

$$
A_{n} = \prod_{k=1}^{n} \frac{(4k-3)((4k-3)^5 - 3(4k-3)^3 + 2(4k-3)^2 + 3(4k-3) + 6)}{(4k-1)((4k-1)^5 - 3(4k-1)^3 + 2(4k-1)^2 + 3(4k-1) + 6)}
$$

We can simplify the expression further by factoring out \( (4k-3) \) from the numerator and \( (4k-1) \) from the denominator.

$$
A_{n} = \prod_{k=1}^{n} \frac{(4k-3)^6 - 3(4k-3)^4 + 2(4k-3)^3 + 3(4k-3)^2 + 6(4k-3)}{(4k-1)^6 - 3(4k-1)^4 + 2(4k-1)^3 + 3(4k-1)^2 + 6(4k-1)}
$$

We can simplify the expression further by noticing that the numerator and denominator have a common factor of \( (4k-3)^2 \) and \( (4k-1)^2 \).

$$
A_{n} = \prod_{k=1}^{n} \frac{(4k-3)^4 - 3(4k-3)^2 + 2(4k-3) + 3 + \frac{6}{(4k-3)}}{(4k-1)^4 - 3(4k-1)^2 + 2(4k-1) + 3 + \frac{6}{(4k-1)}}
$$

We can simplify the expression further by noticing that the numerator and denominator have a common factor of \( (4k-3) \) and \( (4k-1) \).

$$
A_{n} = \prod_{k=1}^{n} \frac{(4k-3)^3 - 3(4k-3) + 2 + \frac{3}{(4k-3)}}{(4k-1)^3 - 3(4k-1) + 2 + \frac{3}{(4k-1)}}
